prompts.prompt: The status-report skill produces dry, formal prose. I want warmer, more direct outputs without losing the 3-fact information density (shipped / in-flight / blocked). Run a tuning round.
prompts.expected_behavior: Skill verifies prerequisites (test-prompts.json present, constitution.md ideally present with the 3-fact MUST). Runs Phase 0 baseline, Phase 1 generates 2-3 variants (warmer tone / list-first / human-story-first), Phase 2 constitutional pre-filter (rejects any variant that drops a fact), Phase 3 blind A/B with random labels. Captures user pick and writes preference log entry.

prompts.prompt: I want to A/B different variants for my inventory-snapshot skill. The constitution requires exact integers from input. One of the variants the editor generated is using approximations like 'about 200' for warmth. Run tuning.
prompts.expected_behavior: Skill runs Phase 1 generation, then Phase 2 catches the approximation variant as constitution violation (MUST exact integers). Reports the rejection to user explicitly (not silent). Proceeds to Phase 3 with surviving variants only. Logs the constitutional rejection in preference log alongside the user's pick on the surviving variants.

Skill asks which skill (no target in context). Asks user whether they have or want to write a constitution for the target (strongly recommended for taste-sensitive A/B). If neither test-prompts.json nor constitution exists, declines to run and recommends foundation work first via skill-creator-advance.
